morning_mobility.title: Daily morning mobility (5–7 min, before coffee)

post_run_mobility.title: Post-run mobility (10 min, non-negotiable on long-run day)

items.name: Foam roller
items.prescription: 90 s per area: glutes, IT band, quads, calves

long_mobility_block.title: Longer mobility block (15–20 min, twice/week — Friday + Sunday)

saturday.label: Short cardio that fits the work window OR rest

climb_quarry.title: Climb #1 — Auburn Quarry rope solo
notes: Bike ride over IS your warm-up (zone 2)
10 min easy traversing / mobile-shoulder warm-up at the wall
Moderate intensity. Avoid max-effort cranking on left arm in compromised positions (deep underclings, gastons, full lock-offs)
Stop while you still have one good lap left — leaving reps on the table is the point right now
Cool-down bike ride; band ER + t-spine extension at home
Weather-blocked if any precip in past 24h OR forecast in next 24h (granite stays slick after rain). Fallback: hangboard_indoor.

exercises.name: Pull-ups
exercises.load: bodyweight; add a 3-sec lower when 3×8 feels easy

run_hilly_z2.title: Run — hilly zone 2
notes: Hilly local terrain, zone 2 (you can hold a conversation)
Add 4× ~20 s strides at the end if feeling fresh
Base-building, not interval day. Resist the urge to push pace.

strength_b.title: Strength B — push / hinge / mobility-biased lower body

exercises.name: Cossack squat
exercises.load: bodyweight; wks 3–4 optional 10–15 lb DB goblet

exercises.name: ATG / Bulgarian split squat
exercises.prescription: 3 × 8 / side, slow eccentric, knee tracks past toe

exercises.name: Single-leg RDL
exercises.load: 20 lb (progress to 25 lb when 8/side feels strong)

exercises.name: DB floor press
exercises.note: limits overhead ROM — safe substitute for bench/OHP

mobility_or_rest.title: Mobility only OR rest
notes: Choose: full mobility block OR fully off if Tue/Thu left you cooked
If mobility: foam roller t-spine + full band shoulder series + hip mobility

long_day.title: Long day (Thu — alternates: Hike OR Climb #2)
notes: Week A: Long hilly hike or 8–10 mi run, 2–4 hr conversational. Light pack → progress to weighted (5/15/25 lb).
Week B: Outdoor tension board 30–45 min, vertical to slight overhang ONLY (not 30°). Followed by 60–90 min easy bike or run.
Alternate weekly until 4 weeks symptom-free at this volume — then make Week B the default and the schedule becomes 2× climbing/wk.
Buffered by Wed Run z2 the day before (light aerobic; legs come in fresh) and Strength B the day after (legs-on-legs — silent downscale Strength B loads if Long Day went hard).
Week B is weather-blocked (tension board is outside). If blocked, fall back to hangboard_indoor + an easy run/bike.

hangboard_indoor.title: Hangboard (indoor — rainy-day climb substitute)
notes: Used when the quarry / outdoor tension board is weather-blocked. 1×/week max, never as an addition to a normal climb week.
Protocol: Density Hangs adapted for rotator cuff rehab (Tyler Nelson Density Hangs + Hooper's Beta active-scap rule + the program's 'no symptoms' gate).
Edge: 20 mm or larger. Half-crimp or open hand. NO full crimp.

Position: two-arm only, bar at standing reach (don't jump to a higher bar that pushes shoulders past 90° overhead). Active scap throughout — shoulders pulled down/back, lats engaged, chest open. NO passive dead-hangs; drop off and reset between every hang.
Bodyweight only in Phase 1 — no added weight.
Hard stop at any shoulder symptom. Pain-monitoring model applies.

exercises.name: Density Hang
exercises.note: 20 mm+ edge · half-crimp or open hand · two-arm at standing reach · active scap throughout (no passive dead-hangs) · stop at any shoulder symptom · Wk 1 dose; progress toward 30s × 6 × 3 over weeks.

weekend_cardio_or_rest.title: Short cardio that fits the work window OR rest
weekend_cardio_or_rest.duration: 60–90 min if work allows; otherwise rest
notes: Erik works most Saturdays through May (lifts in June). Sunday work continues into summer.
Choose: short slow run, easy bike, mobility block, OR rest — whatever the work day leaves room for.
This is bonus aerobic, not the week's main aerobic work — Wed z2 + Thu Long Day cover that.
Auburn hills mean even slow runs can drift into z3 — that's geography, not a violation. Stay in conversational effort.

rest.title: Rest
notes: Full rest day. Light walking is fine. No structured training.
